updated_conclusion: The full provider descriptor setup-function clustering is now exportable as compact worklists. Reverser can emit a small JSON/CSV triage view from pe-provider-descriptor-scan without committing or inspecting the multi-megabyte raw clustered scan.

outputs: cluster JSON retains function ranges, descriptor counts, setup/clone counts, and bounded descriptor previews
cluster CSV flattens one row per setup function for spreadsheet triage
cluster export options imply --include-refs so setup-function ownership is present

new_observations: The compact CSV has exactly one row per setup-function cluster, so the 99-cluster worklist is now easy to sort by descriptor_count.
The largest cluster 0x1400E3AF0-0x1400E4DE4 still stands out as the highest-value unknown provider family, with 41 descriptors.
The compact JSON preserves bounded descriptor previews and RTTI names while avoiding the full raw slot/reference payload.

relationship_to_previous_milestones.provider-descriptor-setup-function-clust: Turns the full clustered scan into a compact worklist artifact suitable for triage and automation.
relationship_to_previous_milestones.login-settings-provider-address-ref-func: Uses the same setup-function ownership model but scales it to all discovered provider rows.

bounded_inference.statement: Provider descriptor reversing can now proceed by sorting and tracing setup-function clusters rather than selecting individual descriptor rows by address. The compact CSV should be the default planning surface for the next pass.
bounded_inference.confidence: high for worklist mechanics, medium for cluster subsystem labels until string/literal enrichment is added

next_targets: Add string/literal enrichment to setup-function clusters to infer subsystem labels from nearby static constants.
Trace setup function 0x1400E3AF0-0x1400E4DE4 because it owns the largest provider descriptor cluster.
Trace shared known cluster 0x1400CCA20-0x1400CCFFC to clarify the directlogin-adjacent and vt-varc/source-provider relationship.
